contentList.content: Difficulty
The classic Home Brew experience.
A narrative adventure placing story over combat. Player Hit Points are doubled. Fine tune NPCs via the Mod Configuration Menu.
A narrative adventure placing story over combat. Player Hit Points are doubled. Fine tune NPCs via the Mod Configuration Menu.
A narrative adventure placing story over combat. Player Hit Points are doubled. Fine tune NPCs via the Mod Configuration Menu.
A narrative adventure placing story over combat. Player Hit Points are doubled. Fine tune NPCs via the Mod Configuration Menu.
A balanced adventure full of challenging choices. Intended for players new to Home Brew. Fine tune NPCs via the Mod Configuration Menu.
A balanced adventure full of challenging choices. Intended for players new to Home Brew. Fine tune NPCs via the Mod Configuration Menu.
A balanced adventure full of challenging choices. Intended for players new to Home Brew. Fine tune NPCs via the Mod Configuration Menu.
A balanced adventure full of challenging choices. Intended for players new to Home Brew. Fine tune NPCs via the Mod Configuration Menu.
A tougher adventure emphasising strategy. Intended for those experienced with Home Brew. Fine tune NPCs via the Mod Configuration Menu.
A tougher adventure emphasising strategy. Intended for those experienced with Home Brew. Fine tune NPCs via the Mod Configuration Menu.
A tougher adventure emphasising strategy. Intended for those experienced with Home Brew. Fine tune NPCs via the Mod Configuration Menu.
A tougher adventure emphasising strategy. Intended for those experienced with Home Brew. Fine tune NPCs via the Mod Configuration Menu.
A deadly adventure emphasizing consequence. Intended for veterans of Home Brew. Fine tune NPCs via the Mod Configuration Menu.
Hall of Heroes
Hall of Heroes: Challenge Details

This is an incredibly intense difficulty recommended only for the most seasoned of Home Brew veterans.

The most dangerous foes you'll encounter are more powerful than ever, boasting new abilities and Legendary Actions. Additionally, you cannot reload older save files if events go awry - your choices and their consequences are permanent. If your entire party is defeated in combat, your Honour mode run will end in failure.

If you are victorious and beat Home Brew on this difficulty, your accomplishment will be listed in game for all other players players to see! Upon having done so, reach out to the mod author (HaVeNII7) with proof to be included.

Good luck, adventurer. You're really going to need it.
Hall of Heroes
A recording of Faerûn's most honourable individuals.
This magical book seems to fill its own pages at rare and uneven intervals. Will you find your own name within, one day?

Let the record show that the following individuals have braved the greatest perils of Faerûn for glory and renown. May their exploits live on forever, a paragon of honour to look to in times of doubt!
Honoured Adventurers: - Captain Swampass - drivaNce - Death - Second-Mate Assburn - Urha - Vee, Chaotic Pontiff of Gremlin Energy - Moorzaube - Mirenna - Gumbych - Steelplayer36 - Greed - Bananella XXIII - Mako - Annoynamous72z - Kaiser Karry, Champion of Lightning
Mythic
A ruthless adventure emphasizing loss. Intended for masters of Home Brew. Fine tune NPCs via the Mod Configuration Menu.
Honoured Myth
A merciless adventure emphasizing suffering. Both Honour and Mythic rulesets are applied. Fine tune NPCs via the Mod Configuration Menu.
Mythic Ruleset
Introduces a variety of new mechanics which hinder the player at every turn.
Hostile characters will be exceedingly strong and much harder to kill in combat.
Exhaustion
Envigoration
Perilous Wound
Toxicity
Destabilized Soul
Demoralized
Morale Boost
Exhaustion I: Winded

Affected creature is winded. Their breathing grows heavier, and strain begins to take hold, though their effectiveness remains intact.

Upon taking a Short Rest or finishing a combat encounter, one stack of Exhaustion will be removed.
Exhaustion II: Faltering

Affected creature is faltering. Their endurance begins to fail, and maintaining peak performance becomes increasingly difficult.

Upon taking a Short Rest or finishing a combat encounter, one stack of Exhaustion will be removed.
Exhaustion III: Drained

Affected creature is drained. Fatigue spreads through their body, warning of an impending physical consequence.

Upon taking a Short Rest or finishing a combat encounter, one stack of Exhaustion will be removed.
Exhaustion IV: Fatigued

Affected creature is fatigued. Dangerous levels of exhaustion have begun to take hold.

They have a -[1] penalty to all Attack Rolls and their physical Ability Scores are reduced by [2].

Upon taking a Short Rest or finishing a combat encounter, one stack of Exhaustion will be removed.
Exhaustion V: Debilitated

Affected creature is debilitated. Their body feels sluggish and addled.

They have a -[1] penalty to all Attack Rolls and their physical Ability Scores are reduced by [2]. In addition, they cannot make Reactions.

Upon taking a Short Rest or finishing a combat encounter, one stack of Exhaustion will be removed.
Exhaustion VI: Crippled

Affected creature is crippled. Their body struggles to respond.

They have a -[1] penalty to all Attack Rolls and their physical Ability Scores are reduced by [2]. In addition, they cannot make Reactions. Furthermore, all Attack Rolls made toward them are rolled with Advantage.

Upon taking a Short Rest or finishing a combat encounter, one stack of Exhaustion will be removed.
Exhaustion VII: Collapsing

Affected creature is in a state of collapse. Systems within their body have begun to give out.

They have a -[1] penalty to all Attack Rolls and their physical Ability Scores are reduced by [2]. In addition, they cannot make Reactions. Furthermore, all Attack Rolls made toward them are rolled with Advantage. Finally, their Movement Speed is reduced by [3]% and their Initiative is reduced by 10.

Upon taking a Short Rest or finishing a combat encounter, one stack of Exhaustion will be removed.
Exhaustion VIII: Broken

Affected creature is broken. Their physical and mental state has deteriorated to the brink of total failure.

They have a -[1] penalty to all Attack Rolls and their physical Ability Scores are reduced by [2]. In addition, they cannot make Reactions. Furthermore, all Attack Rolls made toward them are rolled with Advantage. Finally, their Movement Speed is reduced by [3]%, their Initiative is reduced by 10, and their maximum Hit Points are reduced by [4]%.

Upon taking a Short Rest or finishing a combat encounter, one stack of Exhaustion will be removed.
Exhaustion IX: Losing Consciousness

Affected creature is losing consciousness. Their body begins to shut down as exhaustion overwhelms their ability to remain conscious.

They have a -[1] penalty to all Attack Rolls and their physical Ability Scores are reduced by [2]. In addition, they cannot make Reactions. Furthermore, all Attack Rolls made toward them are rolled with Advantage. Finally, their Movement Speed is reduced by [3]%, their Initiative is reduced by 10, and their maximum Hit Points are reduced by [4]%.

Upon taking a Short Rest or finishing a combat encounter, one stack of Exhaustion will be removed.
Exhaustion X: At Death's Door

Affected creature is now on the brink of death. Their body can no longer sustain life, and catastrophic failure is imminent.

They have a -[1] penalty to all Attack Rolls and their physical Ability Scores are reduced by [2]. In addition, they cannot make Reactions. Furthermore, all Attack Rolls made toward them are rolled with Advantage. Finally, their Movement Speed is reduced by [3]%, their Initiative is reduced by 10, and their maximum Hit Points are reduced by [4]%.

This creature has reached its limit, and has fallen into a Catatonic state. Additional stacks of Exhaustion will immediately kill them.

Upon taking a Short Rest or finishing a combat encounter, one stack of Exhaustion will be removed.
Stress I: Reluctant

Affected creature is feeling reluctant. Their resolve wavers, and doubt begins to creep into their thoughts, though their effectiveness remains intact.

Upon taking a Short Rest or finishing a combat encounter, one stack will be removed. Stress may also be reduced by pressing the advantage in combat.
Stress II: Nervous

Affected creature is feeling nervous. Their composure weakens, and maintaining focus becomes increasingly difficult.

Upon taking a Short Rest or finishing a combat encounter, one stack will be removed. Stress may also be reduced by pressing the advantage in combat.
Stress III: Tense

Affected creature is feeling tense. Psychological strain builds within their mind, warning of an impending mental breakdown.

Upon taking a Short Rest or finishing a combat encounter, one stack will be removed. Stress may also be reduced by pressing the advantage in combat.
Stress IV: Worried

Affected creature is feeling worried. Mounting stress has now begun to undermine their confidence.

They have a -[1] penalty to all Saving Throws and their mental Ability Scores are reduced by [2].

Upon taking a Short Rest or finishing a combat encounter, one stack will be removed. Stress may also be reduced by pressing the advantage in combat.
Stress V: Anxious

Affected creature is feeling anxious. Their thoughts race uncontrollably, disrupting their ability to press on.

They have a -[1] penalty to all Saving Throws and their mental Ability Scores are reduced by [2]. In addition, they cannot utilize their Bonus Action.

Upon taking a Short Rest or finishing a combat encounter, one stack will be removed. Stress may also be reduced by pressing the advantage in combat.
Stress VI: Fractured

Affected creature is feeling fractured. Their mental stability deteriorates, leaving them vulnerable to further breakdown.

They have a -[1] penalty to all Saving Throws and their mental Ability Scores are reduced by [2]. In addition, they cannot utilize their Bonus Action. Furthermore, at the beginning of each turn, their Concentration will be broken.

Upon taking a Short Rest or finishing a combat encounter, one stack will be removed. Stress may also be reduced by pressing the advantage in combat.
Stress VII: Unraveling

Affected creature's psyche is unraveling. Their thoughts fragment, and their ability to respond coherently begins to fail.

They have a -[1] penalty to all Saving Throws and their mental Ability Scores are reduced by [2]. In addition, they cannot utilize their Bonus Action. Furthermore, at the beginning of each turn, their Concentration will be broken and they will take [3].

Upon taking a Short Rest or finishing a combat encounter, one stack will be removed. Stress may also be reduced by pressing the advantage in combat.
Stress VIII: Breaking

Affected creature is nearing psychological collapse. Their will falters as overwhelming stress consumes their ability to function.

They have a -[1] penalty to all Saving Throws and their mental Ability Scores are reduced by [2]. In addition, they cannot utilize their Bonus Action. Furthermore, at the beginning of each turn, their Concentration will be broken and they will take [3]. Finally, they are unable to speak.

Upon taking a Short Rest or finishing a combat encounter, one stack will be removed. Stress may also be reduced by pressing the advantage in combat.
Stress IX: Shattered Psyche

Affected creature's psyche has shattered. Their mind can no longer withstand the overwhelming stress, and their ability to function begins to deteriorate rapidly.

They have a -[1] penalty to all Saving Throws and their mental Ability Scores are reduced by [2]. In addition, they cannot utilize their Bonus Action. Furthermore, at the beginning of each turn, their Concentration will be broken and they will take [3]. Finally, they are unable to speak and may flee in Terror at the beginning of each turn.

Upon taking a Short Rest or finishing a combat encounter, one stack will be removed. Stress may also be reduced by pressing the advantage in combat.
Stress X: At Death's Door

Affected creature's mind has reached its breaking point. Their will to continue has collapsed entirely, and catastrophic psychological failure has taken hold.

They have a -[1] penalty to all Saving Throws and their mental Ability Scores are reduced by [2]. In addition, they cannot utilize their Bonus Action. Furthermore, at the beginning of each turn, their Concentration will be broken and they will take [3]. Finally, they are unable to speak and may flee in Terror at the beginning of each turn.

This creature has reached its psychological limit, and has fallen into a Catatonic state. Additional stacks of Stress will immediately kill them.

Upon taking a Short Rest or finishing a combat encounter, one stack will be removed. Stress may also be reduced by pressing the advantage in combat.
Toxicity I: Tainted

Affected creature's bloodstream carries trace amounts of foreign toxin. Their body detects the contamination, but its natural defenses remain sufficient to maintain normal function.

Upon taking a Short Rest, or upon finishing a combat encounter, one stack will be removed.
Toxicity II: Contaminated

Affected creature's blood is contaminated with accumulating toxins. Their internal systems begin diverting resources to containment, and the chemical burden continues to grow.

Upon taking a Short Rest, or upon finishing a combat encounter, one stack will be removed.
Toxicity III: Saturated

Affected creature's body is saturated with toxic compounds. Their organs strain to process the buildup, and further exposure risks destabilizing their biological equilibrium.

Drinking further potions will cause this creature to take [1].

Upon taking a Short Rest, or upon finishing a combat encounter, one stack will be removed.
Toxicity IV: Nauseated

Affected creature's body begins violently rejecting the toxic accumulation within their system. Their metabolism destabilizes as chemical defenses falter.

Drinking further potions will cause this creature to take [1] and accumulate a stack of Stress.

Upon taking a Short Rest, or upon finishing a combat encounter, one stack will be removed.
Toxicity V: Rejection

Affected creature's biological defenses begin actively rejecting further chemical intrusion. Their bloodstream reacts aggressively to additional toxins, causing immediate harm.

Drinking further potions will cause this creature to take [1], as well as accumulate a stack of Stress and Exhaustion.

Upon taking a Short Rest, or upon finishing a combat encounter, one stack will be removed.
Toxicity VI: Chemical Instability

Affected creature's internal chemistry destabilizes as toxin levels overwhelm regulatory functions. Their organs struggle to maintain equilibrium under the mounting biochemical strain.

Drinking further potions will cause this creature to take [1], accumulate a stack of Stress and Exhaustion, as well as fall Unconscious.

Upon taking a Short Rest, or upon finishing a combat encounter, one stack will be removed.
Toxicity VII: Toxic Shock

Affected creature enters toxic shock as their bloodstream becomes critically compromised. Vital systems begin to falter under the overwhelming chemical assault.

Drinking further potions will cause this creature to take [1], accumulate a stack of Stress and Exhaustion, fall Unconscious, and prevent further healing for three turns.

Upon taking a Short Rest, or upon finishing a combat encounter, one stack will be removed.
Toxicity VIII: Systemic Overload

Affected creature suffers systemic overload as toxin saturation exceeds survivable limits. Biological processes begin to fail as the body loses its ability to regulate internal chemistry.

Drinking further potions will cause this creature to take [1], accumulate a stack of Stress and Exhaustion, fall Unconscious, and permenantly prevent further healing upon digestion.

Upon taking a Short Rest, or upon finishing a combat encounter, one stack will be removed.
Toxicity IX: Organ Failure

Affected creature's organs begin shutting down as toxic compounds flood every system. Circulation spreads irreversible damage, and survival becomes increasingly unlikely.

Drinking further potions will place this creature into a Catatonic state.

Upon taking a Short Rest, or upon finishing a combat encounter, one stack will be removed.
Toxicity X: At Death's Door

Affected creature's body collapses under catastrophic toxic overload. Their bloodstream has become lethally compromised, and further chemical exposure will trigger total biological failure.

Drinking further potions will immediately cause this creature to die.

Upon taking a Short Rest, or upon finishing a combat encounter, one stack will be removed.
Resurrection Limit: Two Remaining
Affected creature's soul has become destabilized. They may be brought back to life using <LSTag Type="Spell" Tooltip="Teleportation_Revivify">Revivify</LSTag> just two more times.
Resurrection Limit: One Remaining
Affected creature's soul has become heavily destabilized. They may be brought back to life using <LSTag Type="Spell" Tooltip="Teleportation_Revivify">Revivify</LSTag> just one more time.
Destabilized Soul

Affected creature's soul has become exceedingly destabilized through multiple resurrections. As a result, resurrecting them through normal means is no longer safe. They may no longer be brought back to life using <LSTag Type="Spell" Tooltip="Teleportation_Revivify">Revivify</LSTag>.
Shattered Forearm
Affected creature has suffered a perilous wound! Their Strength has been reduced by 1.
